name: Minimal Pulse
description: Concentric rings breathing from the center. Low-CPU demo fallback.

name: Topographic Tunnel
description: Infinite forward tunnel of noise-displaced contour rings. Rhythmic, deep.

name: Grid World
description: A field of cells rippling with activation waves + neighbor connections.

name: Parquet Glitch
description: Parquet deformation of rotating tiles, fractured by glitch slices.

name: Torus Field
description: Rotating wireframe tori + spheres in 3D. Sustained, harmonic, evolving.

name: Lichtspiel — Opus III
description: Ruttmann Opus III film instrument: a 3D morphing forward tunnel inside 2D rectilinear forms, weathered by film grain. The grid64/arc2 hero.
sourceLineage: Lichtspiel_v3.pde (faithful WEBGL re-port of the P3D original)

gestural.name: Ruttmann Film Faders + Arc
gestural.summary: Eight grid column-faders sculpt the 3D morphing tunnel; the arc twists the camera + drives the aperture. The palette column and the arc presses burst + advance. Native Grid 64 / Arc 2 — a Grid 128 / Arc 4 lights up 8 more faders + 2 more knobs of the SAME sketch.

grid.effect: fader: speed · radius · undulation · lobes · inner-morph density · rect-form density
select palette (one row each) + spawn a 2D rect burst
grain / vignette damage; bottom row cycles the grain type
extended faders: contrast · camera sway · strands · morph size · vignette · ambient bursts · flicker · ring glow

arc.effect: global tunnel twist / camera orbit
toggle the continuous 3D bulge field (1–4 bulges)
aperture / iris pressure
advance palette + spawn a geometric burst
slow camera orbit / recenter the orbit
grain density / cycle grain type

name: Arc + Grid Combo
description: 16-step grid sequencer flashing scanlines under the play-head while four arc-driven 3D objects spin. The Grid 128 / Arc 4 step-sequencer idiom showcase.

gestural.name: Step Sequencer + Arc Rotation
gestural.summary: A 16-step grid sequencer (8 on a Grid 64): rows 0–5 toggle steps, row 7 cuts the play position / latches a loop. Each lane firing under the play-head flashes a scanline. Arc encoders spin one 3D object each (turn = spin up, press = stop); the rings show rotation phase. On an Arc 2 each encoder covers two objects.

grid.effect: toggle a step on/off at (col, row)
cut the play position to that column
set loop start + end

arc.effect: spin up that object's rotation (impulse → free-wheeling velocity)
stop that object's rotation

name: Pattern Grid World
description: A 3D cube field painted cell-by-cell — brightness picks the lit depth layer — flickering, wired with connection lines, and ringed by an optional pulsing border.

gestural.name: Neural Grid + Hypnotic Border
gestural.summary: A 3D cube grid — un-pressed cells flicker; pressing a cell cycles its brightness 0→15 (and picks its lit depth layer). The arc carries 8 controls across 4 PAGES (on an Arc 2, press both encoders together to advance a page): p1 neuron alpha, p2 background + border, p3 connections, p4 motion + life. Each encoder has a turn (a value) + a press (an action).

grid.effect: cycle that cell's brightness 0→15 + stop its flicker
random brightness flicker (speed = flicker variant)

arc.effect: resting-cube alpha · re-roll the resting colour
active-cube alpha · re-roll the active colour
background brightness · randomise the bg colour
border thickness · cycle the border shape
connection opacity · re-roll the connection colour
connection density · clear all connections
field rotation speed · reset the rotation
connection lifetime · spawn a connection burst

name: PAS Arc + Grid
description: Four 3D objects (icosahedron/sphere/torus/helix/möbius) tumbling on X/Y/Z at fader-set frequencies; arc scales + regenerates them.

gestural.name: Fader-Bank 3D Control
gestural.summary: Four control panels of four vertical faders — each drives one 3D object's X/Y/Z rotation frequency + Z-oscillation. Arc encoders scale each object; press regenerates it. On an Arc 2 each encoder press cycles through two of the four objects.

grid.effect: set X / Y / Z rotation-frequency fader (higher row = faster)
set Z-oscillation amplitude fader

arc.effect: scale that object's size
regenerate that object — new shape, colour, position (+ bg flash)

name: UPF AV — Topographic Planes + Tunnel
description: Four noise-driven topographic plane meshes (swirl / radial-wave disc / layered ridge / ripple) tumbling at fader-set frequencies inside a forward-scrolling infinite noise tunnel; arc scales each plane + regenerates it.

gestural.name: Topographic Planes + Tunnel
gestural.summary: Shares the fader-bank tactile layout with PAS but renders noise-driven topographic plane shapes — swirl, disc, ridge, ripple — over a scrolling infinite noise tunnel. Four control panels of four vertical faders set each plane's X/Y/Z rotation frequency + Z-oscillation. Arc encoders scale each plane; press regenerates it. On an Arc 2 each encoder press cycles through two of the four planes.

grid.effect: set X / Y / Z rotation-frequency fader for that object (higher row = faster)
set Z-oscillation-amplitude fader for that object

arc.effect: scale that plane shape (size grows with the encoder)
regenerate plane: new shape kind + new colour + new position; flashes bg colour

name: Playhead Shapes + Strobe Overlays
description: Four 3D objects (complex torus / wavy torus / oscillating sphere) tumbling and jumping under FOUR fullscreen 2D strobe overlays (diagonal / spiral / hex / oval). Grid faders set each strobe's opacity / gap / randomness / origin; arc encoders drive movement intensity + per-object playhead speed/size; an encoder press regenerates that object + all strobe colours.

gestural.name: Playhead Shapes + Strobe Overlays
gestural.summary: Arc encoders rotate 3D objects with playhead-walked positions. Grid drives 4 fullscreen 2D strobe overlay patterns (diagonal/spiral/hex/oval) with opacity/gap/randomness/origin faders. Each encoder press regenerates that object + all strobe colours + the background. On an Arc 2 each encoder press cycles through two of the four objects.

grid.area: grid cols 0-3 / 4-7 / 8-11 / 12-15 (4 strobe panels), any row
grid.effect: set the strobe param fader to that row (top = max). param is col%4: 0=opacity 1=gap 2=randomness 3=origin/rotation

grid.area: bottom row of any strobe panel
grid.effect: set the strobe parameter to its minimum (a near-zero fader reads as off)

arc.effect: set movement intensity (0..1 = none..max position jumping)
set that object's size + self-walking playhead speed (the ring shows the playhead with quarter markers)
regenerate that object — new shape kind + new colour + new strobe colours + new background

name: Ito Box — Cube Roulette
description: A central velocity-physics "roulette" cube with opposite-face colour pairs over a drifting field of four 3D objects (complex/wavy tori, point-sphere, filament cloud). Arc encoders spin yaw/pitch/roll/zoom; a 4-panel fader grid shapes the background. Grid 128 / Arc 4.

gestural.name: Cube Roulette + Background Field
gestural.summary: Central rotating cube driven by velocity-physics encoders (a turn is an impulse into a damped angular velocity — the "roulette" feel). Grid governs four background objects with per-param vertical faders (opacity / movement / randomness / scale), one 4-column panel per object. On an Arc 2 each encoder press cycles through two of the four colour actions; on a Grid 64 the 16 faders fold into pairs.

grid.effect: set that object's parameter fader (higher row = higher level); the param is col%4: 0=opacity 1=movement 2=randomness 3=scale

arc.effect: add yaw velocity (impulse, then damped — the roulette spin)
add pitch velocity
add roll velocity
add zoom velocity (2× impulse, bounded)
randomise the front/back face colour pair
randomise the top/bottom face colour pair
randomise the left/right face colour pair
randomise the background colour + the texture-field structure

name: Parquet Deformation
description: Two stacked Perlin-deformed meshes shimmer while 10 small 3D solids rotate above. A 16-step grid sequencer where the COLUMN selects a parameter category (colour / deform-speed / brightness / scale) and the ROW multiplies it — steps fire on both press and playback. The Grid 128 / Arc 4 parameter-trigger mesh.

gestural.name: Step Sequencer + Deforming Noise Mesh
gestural.summary: Steps toggle on press AND fire on playback — both events trigger column-block parameter changes. Two layered noise meshes deform continuously while the play-head colours the foreground. Arc adds camera + tilt control to the originally grid-only sketch.

grid.effect: toggle step AND trigger that (row, col) parameter change
col 0-3 = randomise that step's colour; col 4-7 = deform speed × row+1; col 8-11 = brightness × row+1; col 12-15 = scale × row+1
set loop_start on first held press, loop_end on second (auto-swap if reversed)

arc.effect: global zoom / camera distance
background-mesh tilt angle (canonical = π/4)
foreground-mesh tilt angle (canonical = π/3)
vertical position of the 10-object overlay (top..bottom)
reset that channel to mid-range

name: PAS Halloween
description: A 2D motion-trail field of drifting circles/triangles/squares/stars and flickering lines, driven by a grid step sequencer: each firing step re-binds a visual parameter by its column (thickness / rate / size / length) and spawns an element. The Grid 128 / Arc 4 parametric-column-trigger showcase.

gestural.name: Step Sequencer + Parametric Column Triggers
gestural.summary: Distinct from monomeArcgridcombo: the column position of each *active step* sets a visual parameter when the play-head fires it. Grid rows 0-5 toggle steps; on playback a firing step at (row, col) sets that row's parameter (row 0 = line thickness, 1 = line rate, 2 = element size, 3 = line length; rows 4-5 bind nothing) and spawns a drifting element at centre with motion trails. Row 7 cuts the play position / latches a loop. Arc encoders extend windchime's arc-also-required principle (the original was grid-only): enc0 trail strength, enc1 hue shift, enc2 element lifespan, enc3 jitter; on an Arc 2 each encoder folds to cover two of them.

grid.effect: toggle a step on/off at (col, row)
col 0→15 sets that row's parameter (0 = thickness, 1 = line-rate, 2 = element size, 3 = line length); spawns an element
spawn an element only (no parameter bound)
cut the play position to that column
set loop start + end

arc.effect: motion-trail strength (lower = more persistent trails)
hue shift across the active palette
element lifespan multiplier (slower fade)
position-jitter amplitude on drifting elements
clear the field (wipe all elements)
spawn a burst of elements
thin the swarm (cull half)
scatter the elements
